calendar.matters:
Petition For Instructions To Confirm Pro Rate Distribution To Beneficiaries
Off Calendar Jul-12-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Sophia Cizmarik and Danielle Diebert appeared by zoom for U.S. Bank.
Mike Curtis appeared by zoom for Stacy Nakamura (Beneficiary).
Wendy Nakamura appeared by zoom in Propria Persona.

Ms. Nakamura stated that she is struggling, frustrated and overwhelmed with the attorney search. The Court stated that this is the 10th time this petition is on calendar, that Ms. Nakamura has had eight months to find an Attorney, that it is not seeing any progress, that this petition has been pending for twenty months and that it is inclined to take the petition under submission. Ms. Dieter stated that she has nothing to add and that it is appropriate to have the petition decided. Ms. Cizmarik stated that she has nothing to add. Mr. Curtis stated that he supports the petition. Ms. Nakamura stated that she and Stacy are at an impasse and asked the Court for guidance. The Court stated that it will go with option 3 (distribute LLC shares equally to each beneficiary), that it is not in a position to supervise liquidation and that it believes option 3 is an appropriate resolution. The Petition for Instructions to Confirm Pro Rata Distribution to Beneficiaries is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: D. Johnson. Not reported.

Petition For Instructions To Confirm Pro Rate Distribution To Beneficiaries
Off Calendar May-05-2021 Continued To Jun-30-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Sophia Cizmarik, via ZOOM, counsel for U.S. Bank, Petitioner; Dan Cooperider, via ZOOM, counsel for Stacy Nakamura, via ZOOM, Beneficiary; Wendy Nakamura, via ZOOM, in propria persona, interested party.

At the outset, the court queries counsel as to the status of the matter. Ms. Cizmarik reports that the parties are not close to reaching an agreement at this time. The court then turns to Ms. Nakamura and whether she has hired counsel. She reports that due to the pandemic, she has not been able to hire an attorney but does have a list of attorneys which she plans to contact and requests an additional continuance. The court advises Ms. Nakamura that the court will grant one additional continuance but that she will need to file a written objection with the court at least two weeks before the next hearing date, regardless. Otherwise, the court intends to grant the petition under option 3. The court then advises Ms. Cizmarik to submit a new proposed order to the court.

After additional discussion, the Petition for Instructions to Confirm Pro Rata Distributions to Beneficiaries is continued to June 30, 2021 at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition For Instructions To Confirm Pro Rate Distribution To Beneficiaries
Off Calendar Sep-28-2020 Continued To Oct-26-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Sophia Cizmarik appeared by zoom for U.S. Bank (Trustee).
James Lamping appeared by zoom for Wendy Nakamura (Beneficiary).
Michael Curtis appeared by telephone for Stacy Nakamura.

The Court noted that at the last hearing of June 29, 2020 the Parties were encouraged to cooperate and recapped what occurred. Mr. Lamping stated that his Client's position is that this is a terminating trust, that the Former Trustee made some convoluted transactions, that his Client's first preference is to sell everything and distribute the proceeds, and that if they do distribution in kind, the LLC should be dissolved, not outright ownership. Mr. Curtis stated that his Client is opposed to all of that, that she wants the assets in the trust to be distributed and that they see no reason to incur additional expenses to liquidate the LLC. Mr. Curtis also stated that there is no reason for the Trustee and U.S. Bank to be involved in any of that, that a huge capital gains will be involved and that his Client would prefer to do an exchange to reduce the amount of capital gains. The Court questions whether the Parties have tried mediation. Mr. Lamping stated that if they are going to mediation, it will have to happen before distribution, that he will speak with his client about mediation and requested a continuance. Mr. Curtis stated that he has no problem with recommending to his Client to pursue mediation. The Petition for Instructions to Confirm Pro Rata Distribution to Beneficiaries is continued to October 26, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition For Instructions To Confirm Pro Rate Distribution To Beneficiaries
Off Calendar Jun-29-2020 Continued To Sep-28-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Danielle Diebert appeared by telephone for U.S. Bank (Trustee).
James Lamping appeared by telephone for Wendy Nakamura.
Michael Curtis appeared by telephone for Stacy Nakamura.

Mr. Lamping pointed out that the Settlors' intention was for each of the Beneficiaries to receive their equal shares outright, that the LLC was conceived after the death of the Settlors and suggested that the LLC be sold so that this can be done with. Ms. Diebert stated that the prior Trustee created the LLC and that U.S. Bank is trying to wrap this up in the best way possible. The Court raised the issue of the Bank needing to get out of this fight between the Brother and the Sister, raised the issue of the capital gains taxes, stated that it is not clear as to what property Stacy wants to keep, that it is inclined to grant distribution pro rata, and that Stacy and Wendy can figure out what to do from there. Mr. Curtis stated that he agrees with the Court, that each one of them (Wendy and Stacy) can decide if they want to pay taxes, or not, and that they can do a 1031 exchange. Mr. Lamping stated that there is so much capital gains because of delay, that if it is to be distributed outright, the LLC should be dissolved and requested that the Court unwind this mess that the previous Trustee created. Mr. Curtis stated that he is not opposed to Mr. Lamping's suggestion and that it might provide a tax benefit. Ms. Diebert stated that she is willing to go along with whatever the parties can agree on, but it does increase administrative costs, that one of the LLCs holds the mortgage for the Washington property and that the Lender may require that the Beneficiary assume the mortgage. The Petition for Instructions to Confirm Pro Rata Distribution to the Beneficiaries is continued to September 28, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for the parties to determe how to proceed. The Court strongly encourages Wendy and Stacy to be cooperative and work with U.S. Bank.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Cabasares. Not reported.
